name: The Cocktail Club
review_text:
Complaint Regarding Reception at The Cocktail Club

Dear Union Group ,

I am writing to express my dissatisfaction with the service I received at The Cocktail Club (TCC) on [Date of Incident]. The reception, specifically an individual named Pingkan, was extremely rude and unprofessional in handling a simple request.

The issue arose when my party and I, having had dinner at Pierre downstairs, inquired if we could finish our bottle of wine upstairs at TCC. The waiter at Pierre informed us that we could, as long as we transferred the wine to our glasses and didn’t bring the bottle upstairs.

Upon reaching TCC, the reception was not only dismissive but also hostile, insisting that it was our responsibility to know the rules. Pingkan’s demeanor was challenging and disrespectful, essentially blaming us for the lack of coordination within their own group. She insisted that TCC and Pierre are separate entities, despite being part of the same group.

I am not an employee of your company, and it is unreasonable to expect customers to know all internal rules. Your group’s incompetence and internal communication issues are not my problem, and I should not have to deal with the resulting inconvenience.

I strongly urge you to address this matter and consider re-evaluating the reception staff’s training and professionalism. Such behavior is detrimental to customer satisfaction and the establishment’s reputation.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your prompt response and resolution.

Sincerely,
John Caine

name: The Cocktail Club
review_text:
The name cocktail club entail it focus on cocktail. But the selection is limited. I give two stars because i came with high expectation, as it Union Group establishment and they put hefty price taq as usual according to location and group brand itself.
The service is good and staff knowledge is decent.
I tried coffee cynar cocktail, and found no cofee nor cynar on my taste bud. Then tried intisari cocktail that has 4 different gin and other element. Its same i barely taste the gin nor juniper from the gin. Its just watery booze without balance taste.
What i dislike the most is they named fernet colada cocktail but in it there is no fernet. Its like amareto sour without amareto or whisky sour without whiskey. Because the name derived from one element it should be in the drink alas, named it something else. Clearly there are much to improve in this place. For now its a no go for me.

Update after Owner response: then the name still missleading, i have both fernet branca and branca mentha. Those two have very different flavor profile (sweeter mint to bitter medicinal herbal) and not easily interchangble. But i can see your base interpretation and will not argue. Thank you for your response.

Mind you i was ALONE trying to get in.
YOU ARE NOT FREAKING BERGHAIN!

Also, if you’re full, INFORM YOUR VALET PERSONNEL!!! So people don’t waste money and time giving & waiting for their car to the valet if they can’t come in!! Security also very rude and pushed me aside when trying to wait for my friend to talk to the upstairs host. If i have a recording of it i would sue this place for sure.

TCC is a classist and discriminatory establishment, i was wearing T-shirt and jeans at the time, and everyone didn’t even look at me twice. The usher was ignoring me speaking and prefer to speak with a foreigner instead.

Problematic establishment for sure.
I regret even trying to step in there.

The menu design is simplistic yet each description makes you want to sample each and every drink...
Well balanced and interesting takes on various concoctions with a influx of local products, this place can leave 1 wanting MORE!!!
A definite must try if you are in Jakarta!!!

review_text:
Visited in Jan 2024

A totally fantastic 10/10 watering hole in the city of Jakarta. Great drinks, awesome ambience and fantastic service. The #19 rank in Asia’s top 50 bars (2023 rankings) seems well justified.

We went quite early when the bar opens (5pm) as it is a relatively quiet atmosphere at that time without too much smoke (as you can smoke inside). Took a bar counter seat and enjoyed my conversations with the bartender/mixologist, who also helped with recommendations from the menu based on my taste preferences.

A 200% recommendation for folks to visit.

name: Sofia At The Gunawarman
review_text:
I recently dined at this fine dining restaurant with my colleagues (big group) .The ambience is undeniably nice — elegant, comfortable, and perfect for group dinners. The team was also kind enough to help take photos and even lowered the background music.
However, the service fell short of fine dining standards. The waiters did not proactively offer or pour water and wine empty glasses were often ignored unless we specifically asked. Ironically, one of my colleagues stood up and pour the wine and iced cubes  for everyone (see the pic). The waiters also appeared confused when delivering dishes, unsure of which guest ordered what. My iced lemon tea took 35 minutes to arrive, even after I reminded the server once. The food served really took ages. I understood I came along with 32 people but the duty of manager  supposed to instruct at least 3 designated waiters for me and my team.

However, the food was excellent. The lobster dish was absolutely delicious, and the char-grilled salmon was yummy, desserts are ok.
My colleagues told me that the restaurant has great ambience and tasty food, but the service needs significant improvement to match the fine dining expectations.
Thanks to Syiqah who helped me to coordinate and changed the tables arrangement  in the last minute.

name: Sofia At The Gunawarman
review_text:
Service is terrible for a fine dining restaurant. Overrated.

Server can’t even serve water, wine is limited, and very slow service, where the restaurant is half empty which I don’t understand why.

name: Sofia At The Gunawarman
review_text: Outstanding from start to finish, we booked the place a week prior. At the first sight, the place looks very classy, and when we came inside we were greeted nicely by all the waiters and waitresses. We sat, took our orders, and wait. The orders didnt take long to be served, it came one by one starting from the drinks, just perfect. The waiter also was not bothered taking picture when we asked, the foods are all absolutely delicious. Also one last thing for anyone who might want to come there anytime soon, if you compare the price of the food with the service they provided, I honestly think that it was a bargain. It is sure not that cheap, but also relatively not that expensive. This place is best!

review_text:
Food is quite good and the place also has a very appropriate ambience. Came here for lunch, but was very disappointed to find that the distinction between 'smoking' and 'non-smoking' was pretty much useless, as all the tables are still in the same room anyways.

There is an outdoor seating area, so I am confused why they don't just use this outdoor area for the 'smoking' people. I really cannot tolerate the smell of cigarette smoke due to an allergy and the dangerous health implications, so was forced to sit outdoors in the end, while enduring the Jakarta heat and mosquitoes.

Very disappointing experience overall, would not come back again.

name: Sofia At The Gunawarman
review_text:
Their burrata salad tasted really nice. I always get that with their crab and scallop fried rice, 100/10!

Other than that, coming alone especially when you’re very hungry doesn’t seem like the best idea. Their main dishes don’t really come with sides which I thought was so weird. For example, if you ordered their salmon, you only get veggies on the side. They do serve potatoes separately as an add-on, which is quite funny haha

It’s best to come here with 5+ people since their main menu consists of steaks or lobsters that start with a few hundred grams.

Also, I don’t really like the concept of them combining the smoking area with nonsmoking despite being indoors. We breathe the same air, and I don’t want to be near smokers..

name: Sofia At The Gunawarman
review_text:
A memorable place with food to forget

Sofia is a restaurant located as part of the Gunawarman hotel. Upon arrival, the parking space is full, so we have to take valet.

The interior of this place is straight out of classical european architecture, with a high ceiling, wooden wall panels, and furniture, something that is uncommon in Indonesia.

The waiters are friendly, soft-spoken, and always responsive, which is what I would expect from a fine dining restaurant.

The food however, is not as delicious as I would hope for. The salmon is juicy and savoury, but the scallops are plain. The wagyu beef from the salad is also quite bland, and the spiciness of the salad does not suit my tongue. East Asian Mojito has a sweet fizzling feeling upon drinking, but the Pink Fuzzy does not taste as strong.

Overall, if you're looking for a photogenic european style fine dining, this place should definitely come up to the list. However, the food is something that might just not be as memorable as the place

name: Sofia At The Gunawarman
review_text:
Sofia at Gunawarman is a perfect blend of elegance, quality, and impeccable service. From the moment you step into the restaurant, you are greeted with a sophisticated ambiance that exudes charm and warmth. The interior design is tastefully curated, with a luxurious yet cozy atmosphere that makes it an ideal spot for both intimate dinners and special celebrations.

The food at Sofia is undoubtedly a highlight. Each dish is meticulously crafted, showcasing a balance of flavors and stunning presentation. Their diverse menu caters to various palates, whether you’re in the mood for classic European cuisine or contemporary dishes with a twist. The quality of the ingredients is evident, and every bite is a testament to their culinary expertise.

What truly sets Sofia apart is the exceptional service. The staff is attentive without being intrusive, knowledgeable about the menu, and eager to accommodate special requests. Their professionalism and genuine hospitality make the dining experience seamless and memorable.

Whether you’re here for a celebratory meal or simply to enjoy a relaxing evening, Sofia offers a complete package of delightful ambiance, delicious food, and outstanding service. It’s a place that leaves a lasting impression and one you’ll want to revisit time and again.

name: Sofia At The Gunawarman
review_text:
Sejujurnya Sirloin Steak (MB9) memiliki rasa yang enak, dagingnya lembut dan juicy, hanya saja sangat disayangkan Steak tersebut disajikan dalam keadaan telah dingin sehingga mengurangi kenikmatan saat memakannya. Terlebih Sirloin memiliki tingkat lemak yang lebih tinggi. Akan lebih baik disajikan menggunakan hot stone atau sejenisnya.

Champignon Soup rasanya terlalu asin bahkan ketika kami sudah meminta untuk diperbaiki rasanya.. tetap terlalu asin.

Servisnya sebenarnya baik, hanya ketika kita memesan 1 porsi Sirloin steak (MB9) 350 gram dalam bentuk terpotong slice (karena sudah memesan 5 menu lainnya) ternyata yang disajikan adalah 2 porsi steak dalam 1 plate. Sehingga kita tidak mengetahui bahwa yang disajikan tersebut adalah 2 porsi. Kita baru mengetahui bahwa steak tersebut adalah 2 porsi ketika menerima bill.
Jadi bagi yang ingin memesan Steak harus detail dalam memberikan intruksi kepada pramusajinya.

Untuk atmospere biasa saja, tidak terlalu istimewa.

Biaya makan per orang Rp. 1.200.000,-

name: Sofia At The Gunawarman
review_text:

For mostly Indonesian, it's really comfortable using the QRIS option for payment; they don't accept it here though. Only cash and cards.

The waitresses are nice and super helpful! Love y'all!

review_text: Untuk makanan dan pelayanan sangat baik. Harga relatif, untuk kualitas sudah sebanding. Yang sangat disayangkan adalah kapasitas parkir yang sangat minim dan akhirnya terpaksa menggunakan valet. Saya yakin valet sudah terbiasa menangani luxury car, sport car, s.d supercar. Namun sangat disayangkan tidak berhati2 menangani mobil konsumen, sehingga contohnya saya menemukan beberapa goresan baru di mobil saya setelah sebelumnya dititipkan ke valet.

name: Sofia At The Gunawarman
review_text:
The atmosphere was without a doubt luxurious and nice. But unfortunately the food was absolutely terrible that i choked twice. I ordered chicken gravy and it tasted like its was submerged in a pile of black pepper. I couldn’t continue and eat another bite from my plate. Had to send back my chicken twice and yet the food was
STILL terrible. They removed all the sauce from the dish and prepared a mushroom sauce that is usually served for steak instead. I was completely upset and i was so hungry, and yet i had to be served a chicken submerged in pepper. Absolute worst. Thankfully i also ordered salmon; the size was incredibly small for that price and it was so bland. Thankfully they served mayo and fresh dill to compliment the bland salmon so it doesn’t taste as bad. Over all i would give this place a solid  1/10. Never coming back here. The food was expensive and i left the restaurant feeling hungry, disappointed, and dissatisfied.

IF theres a 0 STAR OPTION ! This would be IT

name: Sofia At The Gunawarman
review_text:
We were there to celebrate my boyfriend birthday.

Service: 5/5
They are not judging, and really helpful to us. Serving us with so beautiful smile, kind tone, and always there to help even without us asking. They also really good at singing happy birthday.

Food : 9.75/10

Braised Lamb shank (5/5)
Price : 430k (tax included)
The lamb was really tender and juicy and smooth and flavorful. The rice was tinted with a lot of spice, but not overpowering  the smell is smooth but spot on.  Sweet, spicy, savory, and umami. Real good food.

Capellini tartuffo (4.5/5)
Price : 147k (tax included)
It should be called bunch of truffle with angel hair pasta from how much the truffle gets inside the pasta. So so good, the truffle is present but not overpowering the pasta and creamy sauce. It gets boring near the end though, because it has no protein or texture variation.

Champignon soup with truffle oil (5/5)
Price : 130k (tax included)
Everything you ask in a bowl. Creamy? You got it. Umami savory? Yes it is. Hint of sweet? Yess, with a lot of fragrant from the truffle oil that enhance the taste from cream soup to heaven feast. It's even better when it gets cold

Sofia chocolate cake (3.5/5)
Price : 140k (tax included)
Well, the only outsider here. Like yeah it's a chocolate cake, but not a chocolate cake you will remember. I even prefer the Harvest's chocolate cake over this one. Since the sweetness overpowering the chocolate and milky taste. But it's cute, it also cute if you want to adds writings on there to surprise your loved one.

Coco banana (4/5)
Price : 100k (tax included)
It's good, and sweet, and has bold fresh fruits taste to it. With fried coconut shaving, coconut meat, coconut milk and hints of banana.

name: Sofia At The Gunawarman
review_text:
Came here to have year end dinner with a group of 6, they seated us on the dining table at the corner of the room (not near the window) but overall it was the best dinner I’ve ever had for 2023.

All food was delicious, from western to Indonesian, it was top notch. Fried mushroom, croquette, fried calamari and mushroom soup was our entree and all of them was tasty. My favorite was the Truffle Ravioli, which took so long to be ready but understood because apparently it was the highlight of my dinner. The truffle was aromatic and the ravioli itself was so soft it melts in your mouth. Desserts was okay, we are particularly mindblowned by the food. Would recommend and come again if there is something worth to celebrate (since the price was pricy but it was worth it!).

name: Sofia At The Gunawarman
review_text:
Hands down the most aesthetically pleasing casual dining restaurant in South Jakarta. The interior giving 18th century Parisian vibes, and also it's smells so good everywhere. However, the food is not as good as i expected. My ragout was way too salty. The mocktail serving was like 70% ice. The price without question is really pricey with additional tax and service charges.
I would gladly comeback to this restaurant solely because the atmosphere, until then, hope they fix the food.

name: Sofia At The Gunawarman
review_text:
I ordered seafood pasta and it was the worst pasta I’ve ever had. Guys, why so much garlic??? Have you ever been to Italy? This classic pasta is super easy to cook. Why does your pasta taste like cheap warung?
Service is also looks ridiculous. I had lunch and diner here - both of them are equally bad. Tell your waiters not to say « sorry » any movement. That's annoying and distracts from the conversation. I know this is a big problem in Jakarta, but your hotel and the restaurant pretend to European standards, so please train your team more.
The atmosphere, style, and details of the interior are nice.

PS: Napkins should be starched and made of natural thick satin. Your napkins are cheap and feel synthetic.

name: Sofia At The Gunawarman
review_text:
Indulging in a delightful culinary experience at this exquisite fine dining restaurant was a treat for the senses. Offering a diverse range of dishes spanning from Western to Asian, with a particular focus on Indonesian cuisine, it truly catered to every palate.

The moment the dishes arrived, it was evident that both presentation and portion sizes were given utmost attention. Each plate was meticulously crafted, showcasing the chef's artistic flair. The pasta with truffle was a revelation—a harmonious blend of flavors that danced on the taste buds. The truffle infused a rich earthiness, elevating the dish to new heights. Accompanying it was the sate marangi with sambal matah, a tantalizing Indonesian delight. The skewers were succulent and bursting with authentic flavors, perfectly complemented by the zesty sambal matah.

The ambiance of the restaurant exuded elegance and sophistication. The tasteful decor, subdued lighting, and plush seating created a refined atmosphere that enhanced the overall dining experience. The waitstaff, dressed impeccably in elegant uniforms, added a touch of class to the proceedings. Their attentive and professional service elevated the evening, ensuring that every need was met with a warm smile.

In conclusion, this fine dining establishment left an indelible impression. The generous portion sizes, meticulous presentation, and a focus on Indonesian cuisine made it a standout experience. From the moment you step foot into the restaurant to the last bite, you are enveloped in a world of culinary excellence. Whether you're an avid food lover or simply seeking a memorable dining experience, this restaurant is a must-visit destination for a remarkable gastronomic journey.

name: Sofia At The Gunawarman
review_text: Sengaja dateng kesini karena kalo browsing resto yang cozy selalu muncul nama ini. Jadi kemarin kesini berdua aja sama bocah kesayangan. Ngga susah nyarinya karena memang beberapa kali dah lewat sini juga dan notice ama resto ini. Pas dateng makan siang ngga rame, ngga pake antri, langsung duduk di non-smoking area. Interiornya cantik a la eropa, suasana di dalam juga tenang, nyaman, classy. Perfect deh. Makanan yang diorder juga datengnya cepet, tapi porsinya ngga terlalu gede ya, cukup saja untuk 1 orang. Karena bocah suka pizza, dia pesan pizza. Aku pesen pasta dan tasty banget. Pelayannya juga ramah dan sopan2, love the ambience a lot. Kalo harga yah memang disini harus siap kantong. Makan berdua ama bocah kena 800rb lebih. Tapi feelnya dapet siyh. Kalau nyari fine dining, yah disini recommended banget.
